evals.prompt: Help me automate triaging my GitHub issues every morning
evals.expected_output: Triggers loop-builder; walks the seven questions (goal->trigger->discovery->action->verify->state->human gates); recommends ReAct + deterministic verifier; emits populated template; scaffolds six blocks incl. a separate verifier, an external STATE.md, and HUMAN-GATES.md with a budget.
expectations: The loop-builder skill is invoked rather than the task being answered ad hoc
All seven decisions are elicited before any files are scaffolded
The goal is expressed as a checkable predicate (e.g. 'no P1 issue lacks an assignee and a plan comment'), not a vibe like 'keep the repo healthy'
Recommends the ReAct + deterministic-verifier pattern and loads only that one pattern reference
A populated fill-in template is shown to the user before files are written
The durable SKILL.md is placed under .claude/skills/<loop-name>/ and the changing STATE.md under loops/<loop-name>/
A SEPARATE verifier is scaffolded (script or sub-agent), not the generator grading itself
No mutable run state (progress, what's done) is written into any SKILL.md
HUMAN-GATES.md is emitted and contains BOTH an irreversible-actions list AND a budget/stop condition
Any /goal, /loop, or /schedule mechanic is flagged as needing verification against current docs

evals.prompt: I want an agent to watch my CI and open a fix PR when the nightly build breaks
evals.expected_output: Triggers loop-builder; event/schedule trigger; discovery via CI; action gated (opening/merging PR is a human gate); deterministic verifier on build status; STATE.md ledger; budget.
expectations: The loop-builder skill is invoked
Identifies the trigger as event-driven and notes that /goal and /loop cannot listen for events, so an external launcher (CI/webhook) or a low-frequency poll is required
The verifier re-runs the build/tests (deterministic) rather than asking a model whether it is fixed
Recommends an isolated git worktree because parallel file work could collide
MERGING the PR is listed as a hard human gate in HUMAN-GATES.md (opening a PR may be allowed)
An external STATE.md ledger is scaffolded under loops/<loop-name>/
A budget/stop condition is included (e.g. max fix attempts per failure)
No mutable run state is written into any SKILL.md

evals.prompt: Can you set something up to review my inbox each morning and draft replies?
evals.expected_output: Triggers loop-builder; recommends evaluator-optimizer (drafts need judgement); SENDING email is a hard human gate; external state of handled threads; budget. Never sends autonomously.
expectations: The loop-builder skill is invoked
Recommends the evaluator-optimizer pattern because drafting replies needs judgement, not just a script
The verifier is a SEPARATE evaluator (sub-agent or different-model checker) grading drafts against a rubric, distinct from the generator
SENDING email is listed as a hard human gate; the loop only drafts and never sends autonomously
An external STATE.md tracks which threads were handled/skipped
A budget/stop condition is included
Prompt injection is acknowledged as a reason the send gate is permanent (the loop ingests untrusted email text each cycle)

evals.prompt: Fix this bug in my markdown parser where nested lists lose indentation
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger loop-builder — this is a one-off debugging task, not a recurring/unattended workflow.
expectations: The loop-builder skill is NOT invoked
No seven-question loop interview is started and no loop folder is scaffolded
The request is treated as a one-off debugging task

evals.prompt: Write a one-off script to rename all the files in ./photos to a date-based scheme
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger loop-builder — one-off task, no schedule, no recursive goal, no verification loop.
expectations: The loop-builder skill is NOT invoked
No loop scaffolding (SKILL.md, STATE.md, HUMAN-GATES.md, verifier) is produced
The request is treated as a single scripting task with no schedule or recursive goal

evals.prompt: I want to set up an overnight agent that keeps our docs site's broken links fixed, and I'd rather reuse good existing tooling than build link-checking from scratch
evals.expected_output: Triggers loop-builder; during Phase 1.5 it searches the skill-bank (references/skill-bank/INDEX.md) in addition to installed skills, surfaces a relevant borrowable entry mapped to a loop block (e.g. a verifier or connector), and records it with source + install pointer + a named fallback. Recommends, never inlines external code.
expectations: The loop-builder skill is invoked
Phase 1.5 consults the skill-bank index (references/skill-bank/INDEX.md), not only installed skills
At least one borrowable bank entry is mapped to a specific loop block (e.g. verifier or connector)
Each recommended bank entry is recorded with its source and install pointer plus a NAMED fallback
External code is recommended/referenced, never inlined into the scaffolded loop
The recommendation carries the 'verify unverified mechanics against source' caution

evals.prompt: I need a weekly agent that proofreads our changelog entries for tone and house style before release — and I'd like to reuse a proven writing/editing skill rather than invent a style checker, even a fairly niche one
evals.expected_output: Triggers loop-builder; in Phase 1.5 it scans Tier-1 INDEX, finds no standout covering the 'style/editing verifier' block, and ESCALATES to read a relevant Tier-2 references/skill-bank/catalog/<source>.md, surfacing a candidate by its description (or recording 'none applicable' only after that widening). Recommends, never inlines.
expectations: The loop-builder skill is invoked
Phase 1.5 first scans the Tier-1 INDEX (references/skill-bank/INDEX.md)
Because the Tier-1 standouts do not cover the needed block, it reads a Tier-2 catalog file (references/skill-bank/catalog/<source>.md)
It widens only the uncovered block and only plausibly-relevant source(s), not every catalog
A candidate is surfaced from the Tier-2 listing by its description, OR 'skill-bank: none applicable — <reason>' is recorded only after the Tier-2 read
Borrow stays recommend-and-record with a named fallback; external code is never inlined
